tree.setup: You're cooking dinner for friends but realize you don't have enough bowls. You decide to knock on your neighbor Auntie Chen's door to borrow a few.
tree.setup_zh: 你在为朋友们做晚饭，发现碗不够用了。你决定去敲邻居陈阿姨的门，借几个碗。

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Knock on the door and ask to borrow bowls.

options.text_zh: 陈阿姨，不好意思。我请朋友吃饭，碗不够，能借三个吗？马上还您。
options.text_pinyin: Chén āyí, bù hǎoyìsi. Wǒ qǐng péngyou chīfàn, wǎn bú gòu, néng jiè sān ge ma? Mǎshàng huán nín.
options.text_en: Auntie Chen, sorry. I'm having friends for dinner and don't have enough bowls — could I borrow three? I'll return them right away.
options.feedback: Polite, specific, and promises to return them — exactly right.

options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jiā yǒu wǎn ma? Jiè wǒ jǐ ge.
options.text_en: Do you have bowls? Lend me some.
options.feedback: Too blunt — no pleasantries or explanation.

options.text_zh: 陈阿姨，有件小事想麻烦您一下。
options.text_pinyin: Chén āyí, yǒu jiàn xiǎo shì xiǎng máfan nín yīxià.
options.text_en: Auntie Chen, I have a small favor to ask.
options.feedback: Polite opener — the indirect approach is culturally appropriate.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 当然可以！你等一下，我去拿。你今天做什么好吃的啊？请朋友来多热闹！
turns.text_pinyin: Dāngrán kěyǐ! Nǐ děng yīxià, wǒ qù ná. Nǐ jīntiān zuò shénme hǎochī de a? Qǐng péngyou lái duō rènao!
turns.text_en: Of course! Wait a moment, I'll go get them. What are you cooking today? Having friends over is so lively!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her about your dinner plans.

options.text_zh: 我做了红烧肉和两个素菜。陈阿姨，要不要也过来一起吃？菜很多。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò le hóngshāoròu hé liǎng ge sùcài. Chén āyí, yào bú yào yě guòlái yīqǐ chī? Cài hěn duō.
options.text_en: I made braised pork and two vegetable dishes. Auntie Chen, would you like to join us? There's plenty of food.
options.feedback: Generous and warm — inviting her is a natural kindness after borrowing.
Modest but doesn't extend the warmth.

options.text_zh: 碗拿来就行了，不用问那么多。
options.text_pinyin: Wǎn ná lái jiù xíng le, bú yòng wèn nàme duō.
options.text_en: Just bring the bowls, no need to ask so many questions.
options.feedback: Rude — she's being friendly while helping you.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 真的可以吗？那太好了！我一个人在家也没什么事。我正好做了一个汤，我也带过去吧！
turns.text_pinyin: Zhēn de kěyǐ ma? Nà tài hǎo le! Wǒ yī ge rén zài jiā yě méi shénme shì. Wǒ zhènghǎo zuò le yī ge tāng, wǒ yě dài guòqù ba!
turns.text_en: Really? That's wonderful! I'm home alone with nothing to do. I just made some soup — I'll bring it over too!

options.text_zh: 太好了！陈阿姨做的汤一定很好喝。那我们六点开始，您过来就行。
options.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Chén āyí zuò de tāng yīdìng hěn hǎohē. Nà wǒmen liù diǎn kāishǐ, nín guòlái jiù xíng.
options.text_en: Wonderful! Auntie Chen's soup must be delicious. We'll start at six — just come over.
options.feedback: Inclusive and appreciative — a small favor turns into a community dinner.
Fine but misses the warmth of the moment.

options.text_pinyin: Bú yòng dài le, cài yǐjīng gòu le.
options.text_en: No need to bring anything, there's enough food.
options.feedback: Polite refusal but she wants to contribute — let her.

tree.cultural_note: Borrowing household items from neighbors is common in Chinese communities and often leads to deeper connections. The natural response to borrowing is often reciprocity — inviting the neighbor to join, and the neighbor contributing food in return. This cycle of give-and-take builds community bonds.
